baseline_h3_required_gates.name: public_origin_https_ok
baseline_h3_required_gates.accept_when: HTTPS reachability is true and the target origin is controlled by the experiment.
baseline_h3_required_gates.why: A browser CM row is meaningless if the public origin itself is not reachable.

baseline_h3_required_gates.name: public_origin_alt_svc_h3
baseline_h3_required_gates.accept_when: Alt-Svc advertises h3 for the same WebPKI origin.
baseline_h3_required_gates.why: Chrome must be offered an ordinary HTTP/3 route without forced local-only overrides.

baseline_h3_required_gates.name: server_application_h3_confirmed
baseline_h3_required_gates.accept_when: Server request log and qlog show application HTTP/3 for the target workload.
baseline_h3_required_gates.why: H3 discovery alone is not enough; the application request must actually use HTTP/3.

baseline_h3_required_gates.name: chrome_target_quic_session_observed
baseline_h3_required_gates.accept_when: Chrome NetLog contains target-origin QUIC session or application QUIC job evidence.
baseline_h3_required_gates.why: Server-side H3 should be paired with browser-side attribution when Chrome is the client.

baseline_h3_required_gates.name: expected_workload_requests_reached
baseline_h3_required_gates.accept_when: Expected workload request count is reached for the selected route.
baseline_h3_required_gates.why: A partial request sequence should not be promoted to workload continuity evidence.

active_strong_cm_required_gates.name: application_completion_metric_true
active_strong_cm_required_gates.accept_when: The workload-specific completion metric is true.
active_strong_cm_required_gates.why: Connection continuity claims must not ignore whether the user-visible task completed.

active_strong_cm_required_gates.name: network_change_command_executed
active_strong_cm_required_gates.accept_when: The non-iPhone network-change command is present and exits 0 or an explicitly accepted no-exit condition.
active_strong_cm_required_gates.why: No-change rows are controls, not active migration trials.

active_strong_cm_required_gates.name: client_active_path_changed
active_strong_cm_required_gates.accept_when: Before/after route snapshots classify an active client path change.
active_strong_cm_required_gates.why: Server tuple movement alone can be misleading without client-side path evidence.

active_strong_cm_required_gates.name: server_target_h3_tuple_changed
active_strong_cm_required_gates.accept_when: Target H3 remote tuple count is greater than one for the workload.
active_strong_cm_required_gates.why: A single observed tuple cannot prove path migration at the server endpoint.

active_strong_cm_required_gates.name: server_qlog_path_validation
active_strong_cm_required_gates.accept_when: PATH_CHALLENGE and/or PATH_RESPONSE evidence is present for the active row.
active_strong_cm_required_gates.why: Tuple changes without QUIC path validation are not enough for CM.

active_strong_cm_required_gates.name: chrome_single_target_quic_session
active_strong_cm_required_gates.accept_when: Target-origin Chrome QUIC session count is exactly one.
active_strong_cm_required_gates.why: Multiple target sessions indicate reconnect/session churn rather than single-session CM.

result_classes.accept_when: baseline_h3 gates pass and no active network-change command is part of the row.
result_classes.safe_claim: The controlled public origin can serve the selected Chrome workload over HTTP/3.
result_classes.do_not_claim: Do not claim Connection Migration from a no-change or baseline row.

result_classes.accept_when: All active_strong_cm gates pass in the same row.
result_classes.safe_claim: For this controlled public Chrome workload, task completion coincided with client path change, server tuple change, qlog path validation, and a single Chrome target QUIC session.
result_classes.do_not_claim: Do not generalize to mobile Wi-Fi/LTE, other browsers, CDNs, or all workloads.

result_classes.accept_when: Application completion is true but Chrome target sessions are greater than one, retry is used, or qlog path validation is absent.
result_classes.safe_claim: The application task recovered or completed under disruption.
result_classes.do_not_claim: Do not describe this as single-session QUIC Connection Migration.

result_classes.accept_when: The row maps to a PASS_NEGATIVE_CONTROL classification from the source classifier.
result_classes.safe_claim: The row documents a missing gate or conservative failure mode.
result_classes.do_not_claim: Do not count it as public browser CM success.

result_classes.accept_when: The row lacks the H3 precondition, server artifact, or workload completion evidence needed for interpretation.
result_classes.safe_claim: No paper result claim should be made from this row.
result_classes.do_not_claim: Do not use incomplete infrastructure rows as CM evidence.

workload_rules.primary_use: First active public workload because byte accounting and completion are crisp.
workload_rules.strong_cm_extra_requirement: Range complete, expected byte count reached, no application retry, and one Chrome target QUIC session.
workload_rules.fallback_interpretation: If range completes with multiple sessions or no qlog path validation, report recovery/reconnect rather than CM.

workload_rules.primary_use: Second active public workload because it tests client-sending continuity.
workload_rules.strong_cm_extra_requirement: Upload complete, received bytes match intended payload, and packet/path evidence is not inferred from request tuple alone.
workload_rules.fallback_interpretation: If upload completes but request-level tuple stays one, use qlog/NetLog/proxy evidence before making any path claim.

workload_rules.primary_use: QoE workload that can hide disruption behind startup buffer or rebuffering.
workload_rules.strong_cm_extra_requirement: Playback complete plus startup delay, rebuffer count, target session count, tuple change, and qlog path validation.
workload_rules.fallback_interpretation: Playback completion alone is QoE/recovery evidence, not CM.

workload_rules.primary_use: Streaming-like boundary workload that separates retry recovery from transport continuity.
workload_rules.strong_cm_extra_requirement: Segment completion without retry/reconnect and with all active strong CM gates in one row.
workload_rules.fallback_interpretation: Retry-based segment completion should be framed as application recovery.

safe_boundary.baseline: A public H3 baseline proves only that the controlled origin and Chrome can speak HTTP/3 for the workload.
safe_boundary.active: A strong active CM row requires application completion, client active path change, server target tuple change, qlog path validation, and exactly one Chrome target QUIC session in the same row.
safe_boundary.streaming: Streaming completion must be reported with QoE and session attribution; completion alone is not CM.

paper_use: Run the source classifier on each future controlled-public Chrome row, then use this contract to decide whether the row is baseline evidence, strong single-session CM evidence, task recovery, or a negative control.
